title: These 3 Beaten-Down Stocks Just Saw $25 Million in Insider Buying
author: Leo Miller
description: Three beaten-down stocks across entertainment, payments, and apparel sectors have attracted over $25 million in insider buying in Q2 2026. TKO Group ($4.5M insider buys), Shift4 Payments ($16M from founder Jared Isaacman), and ON Running (ON) ($6.59M from three insiders) are seeing confidence from company insiders despite recent share price declines of 5-55%. The insider purchases suggest these executives believe their stocks are undervalued relative to intrinsic value.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock down 5% in 2026 and 15% from all-time high, but insiders bought $4.5M in Q2 2026 (first insider buys since Q2 2025). Company added $1B in buyback capacity and believes stock is dislocated from intrinsic value, signaling management confidence.

Stock crushed down 55% since start of 2025, but founder Jared Isaacman displayed significant confidence by purchasing $16M in Q2 2026 stock. No insider selling tracked since Q4 2025. Despite acquisition-driven growth concerns, insider buying suggests founder believes in company's value.

Stock crushed down 55% since start of 2025, but founder Jared Isaacman displayed significant confidence by purchasing $16M in Q2 2026 stock. No insider selling tracked since Q4 2025. Despite acquisition-driven growth concerns, insider buying suggests founder believes in company's value.

title: Morgan & Morgan Launches WrestleMania® 42 Sweepstakes With WWE
author: Not Specified
description: Morgan & Morgan, America's largest injury law firm, announced a national sweepstakes partnership with WWE offering one fan two tickets to each night of WrestleMania 42 plus $2,000 in travel expenses. The sweepstakes reinforces Morgan & Morgan's position as WWE's Official Law Firm Partner and aims to engage with WWE's passionate fan base.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: WWE benefits from the partnership through increased promotional reach and brand association with a major law firm, enhancing the prestige of WrestleMania 42 and demonstrating strong commercial partnerships that add value to the event.

title: These 3 Stocks Boosting Buybacks Have Rallying Potential
author: Leo Miller
description: Three major companies - Workday, Chipotle, and TKO Group - are expanding their stock buyback programs, signaling management confidence in their current stock valuations and potential future growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Increased buyback authorization to $5 billion, representing 8% of market cap, with plans to repurchase shares through fiscal 2027. Stock has fallen 18% in 2025, suggesting management sees significant value at current prices.

Added $500 million share repurchase authorization, increasing quarterly buyback spending from $190 million to $465 million. Stock down over 20% in past year, indicating potential value at current price levels.

Planned $1 billion buyback program (4% of market cap), with $800 million through accelerated repurchase. Trading near all-time highs, with expected earnings growth in 2026 due to major media deals for WWE and UFC.

title: A Warner Bros-Paramount Combo Could Create Sports Juggernaut With MLB, NBA, NFL, NHL Rights
author: Chris Katje
description: Warner Bros. Discovery and Paramount Skydance are exploring a potential merger that could create a major sports media platform with comprehensive rights to MLB, NBA, NFL, and NHL games, potentially challenging ESPN's dominance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock experienced significant gains (29% single-day increase), potential merger could expand sports media portfolio and create new competitive opportunities
Potential competitive pressure from proposed merger, but maintaining strong ESPN sports rights portfolio
Recently sold UFC rights to Paramount Skydance, no significant direct impact from proposed merger

title: 3 Big Dividend Hikes Hit the Market—1 Just Doubled Its Payout
author: Leo Miller
description: Three major companies announced significant dividend increases, with TKO Group doubling its dividend following lucrative media rights deals for WWE and UFC, while Lam Research and Intuit also raised their quarterly dividends.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Doubled quarterly dividend, secured massive media rights deals with ESPN and Paramount Skydance, announced $2 billion share buyback program, and demonstrated strong revenue growth
Increased quarterly dividend by 13%, ranks in top 20 dividend yields for semiconductor stocks, has significant market presence in chip manufacturing equipment
Raised quarterly dividend by 15%, notable because most software stocks do not pay dividends, part of top 10 most valuable software companies
